meaning: n.砰然落下,拍击声,失败vi.扑拍,跳动,脚步沉重地走,笨重地摔,猛落vt.笨拙地抛下adv.噗地一声,恰巧
examples: It is the public who decide whether a film is a hit or a flop.
He seemed so sure of his decision, how could he flip-flop so dramatically now?
Julia Gillard pronounced Australia and America great mates, but by the standards of a world-transforming president with a Nobel prize, the trip was a flop.
Casting the right spell can often mean the difference between a flier and a flop.
In fact, it already endured a major flop the day John Carter was released.
